pattern: Count pairs (i < j) where words[i] is both a prefix and suffix of words[j]; at large scale, use a trie keyed on zipped (prefix_char, suffix_char) pairs to avoid O(n²L) brute force.
hints: Pair up the first and last characters of words[i] simultaneously: if words[i] has length L, its 'signature' is [(w[0],w[L-1]), (w[1],w[L-2]), ...]; a word words[j] is a candidate only if words[j] starts with the same character-pair sequence from both ends.
Build a trie where each node stores the count of words whose zipped signature ends at that node; for each new word words[j] walk its own signature through the trie and accumulate counts.
The key insight is that checking isPrefixAndSuffix is equivalent to verifying the length-L zipped sequence of words[i] is a prefix of the zipped sequence of words[j].

edge_inputs.description: two words, words[0] is prefix-suffix of words[1]
two words of equal length — only match if identical
longer word before shorter — no match
all single-character words that match each other
prefix matches but suffix does not
suffix matches but prefix does not
multiple matches in a dense set

stress.description: medium array of random short words from small alphabet to create many matches
